response:
# Review verdict — review-01-messy-ts-app

Total: **37 findings** (4 high, 15 medium, 18 low) over an empty baseline. Treating this as a single PR that introduces everything at once.

## 🛑 Block merge (7)

Findings that either don't work outside the author's machine, encode a real correctness bug, or carry enough agent-risk that landing them sets a bad precedent for the repo's first commit.

| ID | Charge | Why block |
|---|---|---|
| `crime_00007` | Localhost-on-Disk (`/Users/andrew/…`) — med, agent_risk 0.61 | Severity rating understates impact: this literal **cannot work** for any other dev, CI runner, or deploy. Fix is one line. |
| `crime_00006` | Dev-Server URL (`localhost:3000`) — med, agent_risk 0.61 | Same class of bug: silently fails in every non-local environment. Must be env-driven before merge. |
| `crime_00004` | Half-UTC, Half-Local — **high**, agent_risk 0.68 | An actual correctness bug — same receiver `d` mixes `getUTCFullYear()` and `getMonth()`, will skew by the host's TZ offset. |
| `crime_00010` | Timezone Roulette — med, agent_risk 0.61 | `new Date("2026-12-25T07:00:00")` and `"2026-12-20"` parse against host TZ. With `crime_00004` in the same file, the date module is unfixable until both are resolved. |
| `crime_00011` | Test That Proves Nothing — med, agent_risk 0.46 | Zero `expect` calls in "renders the enterprise plan". A test that asserts nothing is worse than no test; it creates false confidence for human reviewers and agents alike. |
| `crime_00002` | God Function `generateInvoice` (204 lines) — **high**, agent_risk 0.63 | 3.4× the threshold, sitting in a billing module. Any agent edit later is a coin-flip; better to split before this lands than to retrofit it after callers form around it. |
| `crime_00013` | `calculateRefundPreview` does refunds & emails — med, agent_risk 0.52 | Name promises pure preview, body calls `saveRefundAudit` + `sendRefundEmail`. This is exactly the kind of foot-gun an agent will pull mid-flight. |

## ⚠️ Warn (15)

Real issues — author should fix before or shortly after merge, but they don't block a first-pass review.

| ID | Charge | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `crime_00001` | SVG With Embedded Raster — **high**, agent_risk 0.7 | High severity but cosmetic; warn unless asset is in the rendering hot path. |
| `crime_00003` | Temporal Recklessness in `src/date.ts` (8 calls) — **high**, agent_risk 0.69 | The whole module needs a clock abstraction; flag as a follow-up issue rather than blocking this PR. |
| `crime_00009` | Temporal Recklessness in `billing.ts` (7 calls) — med, agent_risk 0.53 | Same class of fix; track with `crime_00003`. |
| `crime_00005` | Missing Agent Context — med, agent_risk 0.61 | Add `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` before more agent work lands; non-blocking for the code itself. |
| `crime_00014` | DST-Naive Day Math (`+ 86400000`) — med, agent_risk 0.59 | Real bug class but isolated to one line; fix in the date-module follow-up. |
| `crime_00017` | Logic in the Alibi (refund auth rule in a comment) — med, agent_risk 0.5 | Encode the rule next to or after the refund refactor (`crime_00013`). |
| `crime_00012` | "enterprise" literal across 4 files — med, agent_risk 0.6 | Centralise before the 5th copy appears. |
| `crime_00015` | Tenant/account/organisation/team/workspace drift — med, agent_risk 0.58 | Pick the canonical noun before adding more routes. |
| `crime_00018` | Duplicated Nav Source (`Plans` vs `Billing`) — med, agent_risk 0.57 | Pairs with `crime_00020`; fix together. |
| `crime_00020` | Route Metadata Drift on `/settings/billing` — med, agent_risk 0.56 | Four names for one destination; align in the nav cleanup. |
| `crime_00019` | TODO density in `src/todo.ts` (14 markers in 33 lines) — med, agent_risk 0.57 | This is mostly a fixture artefact, but if it's intended to ship, triage before merging. |
| `crime_00016` | God File `billing.ts` (310 lines) — med, agent_risk 0.5 | Just past the threshold; will follow from splitting `generateInvoice`. |
| `crime_00021` | Oversized Raster (253 KB hero) — low, agent_risk 0.52 | Page-weight concern; cheap to fix. |
| `crime_00022` | Docs-Code Drift (`./setup.md` missing) — low, agent_risk 0.51 | Fix the link or restore the file. |
| `crime_00023` | Sync I/O in Hot Path (3 `*Sync` calls in `loadPlanSchema`) — low, agent_risk 0.51 | Confidence 0.9 — clear async swap, but blast_radius is 0 here. |

## 🤫 Ignore for this PR (15)

Low severity AND agent_risk ≤ 0.51. Stylistic, ergonomic, or local — accumulate them on a backlog ticket, don't gate this merge.

| ID | Charge | Why ignore |
|---|---|---|
| `crime_00024` | Temporal Recklessness — 1 `Date.now()` in `state.ts`, agent_risk 0.51 | Covered by the date-module follow-up. |
| `crime_00025` | Icon-Sized Raster (32×32 check icon) — agent_risk 0.5 | Polish, not a risk. |
| `crime_00026` | Date String Sewing — agent_risk 0.5 | Subsumed by the date-module fix. |
| `crime_00027` | Host-Locale Drift (`toLocaleDateString()`) — agent_risk 0.5 | Same. |
| `crime_00028` | Unprefixed booleans in `state.ts` — agent_risk 0.49 | Naming nit. |
| `crime_00029` | Unprefixed booleans in `petty.ts` — agent_risk 0.49 | Same. |
| `crime_00030` | Test with weak matcher (`creates an invoice`) — agent_risk 0.34 | Strengthen when the test next changes. |
| `crime_00031` | Option-bag `describeCheckout` — agent_risk 0.48 | Type-shape nit. |
| `crime_00032` | Return-shape roulette `resolvePaymentResult` — agent_risk 0.48 | Same. |
| `crime_00033` | Negative-flag maze `!disableBilling && !skipRetry` — agent_risk 0.47 | Rename next time these flags change. |
| `crime_00034` | Plural mismatch (`users: User`, `invoice: Invoice[]`) — agent_risk 0.47 | Likely a typo; one-line fix when convenient. |
| `crime_00035` | TODO density in `billing.ts` (just over threshold) — agent_risk 0.4 | Triage with `crime_00019`. |
| `crime_00036`–`38` | Orphaned destinations (`/account/subscription`, `/team`, `/workspace/members`) — agent_risk 0.46 each | Confidence 0.65 — could be in-flight pages. Ask the author rather than blocking. |

## Summary
Block on the 7 things that won't work elsewhere or actively mislead readers. Warn on the 15 follow-ups (cluster them: one date-module ticket, one nav/routing ticket, one billing-refactor ticket). Ignore the rest for this review — they're noise unless `crimes scan` gets re-run after the blockers are cleared.
